hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will improve SSTR2 selectivity by forming specific π-π stacking or hydrophobic interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), which are unique to SSTR2. Simultaneously, mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will enhance steric complementarity with SSTR2-specific TM6 (272/273/276/279) and reduce off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong SSTR2 binding (ddG) but lack structural integrity and docking quality. To improve selectivity, we must focus on disrupting off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions while maintaining SSTR2 pharmacophore interactions.

proposed_changes.rationale: Low pLDDT and docking scores suggest poor structural quality. Tightening clash constraints will enforce better geometry and improve docking performance.
To improve selectivity, we must disrupt off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) while preserving key pharmacophore residues (FWKT at 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
